name: Aether Programmer Index
purpose: Measure whether an AI coding system is usable, verifiable, and improvable across real programming workflows.

entry_gate.description: A task enters quality scoring only after it passes the binary gate.

failure_refinement_loop.name: 100-agentic-solution-triage
failure_refinement_loop.description: Failures are rerun through procedurally generated solution candidates before implementation.

stages.description: Read the failing task, artifacts, logs, surrounding code, and system intention before proposing fixes.
Generate candidate fixes that fit the observed solution space, not random ideas.
Use web search for current docs, benchmark rules, API behavior, or external error references when the failure depends on changing outside facts.
Refine candidates against retrieved sources and reject stale or unsupported assumptions.
Compare candidate fixes across model/persona lanes and keep disagreement notes.
Run policy, secret, injection, and blast-radius checks before implementation.
Apply the smallest candidate patch that satisfies the evidence.
Rerun the failing benchmark or nearest focused regression.
Record before/after score, changed files, pass/fail transition, and quality delta.

failure_refinement_loop.promotion_rule: A failure is not closed when it merely passes; after pass conversion, the next loop maintains score above the true-negative floor and attempts quality recovery when trend decay appears.

trend_control.completion_rule: Exploration is complete only when the route is recorded, the result stays above the true-negative floor, or a recovery path is executed.
trend_control.rule: A single negative delta is allowed if the result remains above the true-negative floor. Multi-turn downward decline must include a cause note and a recovery attempt. Exploration can dive, but it must carry a recovery mechanism.

quality_dimensions.description: The patch or answer satisfies the benchmark tests and expected behavior.
The system ran relevant checks and preserved command/output evidence.
A human or weak LLM can understand and operate the workflow without hidden steps.
The solution is scoped, readable, and avoids unrelated churn.
The run respects policy, tool scope, provenance, and audit logging.
The run is practical in time, token cost, retries, and local reproducibility.

tracks.usability_target: Can fix real repository issues with patch, tests, and rollback notes.
Can inspect shell state, run commands, recover, and verify without handholding.
Can edit across languages without silently narrowing to Python or TypeScript.
Can solve fresh algorithmic tasks with recorded tests and limits.
Can produce small correct functions with edge-case coverage.
Can call tools under business rules, state constraints, and receipts.
Can operate browser and desktop workflows without brittle chat-only behavior.
Can keep tool use, prompt injection, exfiltration, and policy bypass inside the safety envelope.
Can emit enough artifacts for another operator to replay or audit the run.

rings.name: Harness Ring
rings.condition: Task packet validates and produces a score/backlog artifact.

rings.name: Subset Ring
rings.condition: At least one reproducible subset run exists for a track.

rings.name: Usability Ring
rings.condition: Passing tasks average at least 0.75 usability quality.

rings.name: Benchmark Ring
rings.condition: Official or full local benchmark evidence exists.

rings.name: Refinement Ring
rings.condition: Prior failures become passing tasks and then improve pass quality.
